title: Discovery Service: OpenTelemetry Instrumentation, Prometheus Metrics, and Health Endpoints
description: Instrument the Discovery Service with distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry → OTLP export) and metrics (Prometheus /metrics endpoint). Add manual spans for all security-critical operations. Define a complete metrics catalogue covering gateway KPIs and security KPIs. Implement deep health endpoints for Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes. All labels must be designed to avoid high cardinality.
objectives: Complete request tracing from HTTP boundary through DB and chain operations with security context in spans
Prometheus metrics for SRE alerting and security dashboards
Health endpoints that correctly reflect DB, Redis, and chain dependency state

steps: Step 1 - OTel SDK Setup: Install opentelemetry-sdk, opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi, opentelemetry-instrumentation-sqlalchemy, opentelemetry-instrumentation-httpx, opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-grpc. Initialize in lifespan startup: TracerProvider with OTLPSpanExporter(endpoint=settings.otlp_endpoint). Configure BatchSpanProcessor. Set resource attributes: service.name='discovery-service', service.version=app_version, deployment.environment=settings.env.

Step 2 - FastAPI Auto-Instrumentation: Call FastAPIInstrumentor.instrument_app(app, excluded_urls='/health/live,/health/ready,/metrics'). This auto-creates spans for all HTTP requests. Exclude health/metrics endpoints from tracing noise.

Step 3 - SQLAlchemy Auto-Instrumentation: Call SQLAlchemyInstrumentor().instrument(engine=async_engine). This adds spans for all DB queries. Enable: sanitize_sql=True to redact parameter values from span attributes. Enable: enable_commenter=True for sqlcommenter format in query logs.

Step 4 - Manual Spans for Security Operations: In each of the following operations, create a child span with attributes: (a) issue_credential: span name='credential.issue', attributes: credential_type, env, subject_did (truncated), status. (b) revoke_credential: span name='credential.revoke', attributes: credential_type, env, reason, severity. (c) publish_status_list: span name='status_list.publish', attributes: status_list_id, env, anchored_on_chain. (d) sentinel_onboard: span name='sentinel.onboard', attributes: role, env. (e) chain_anchor: span name='chain.anchor', attributes: contract, tx_hash. No DID full strings in span labels — use hash prefix (first 12 chars).

Step 5 - Prometheus Metrics: Create services/discovery/src/discovery/metrics.py using prometheus_client. Define: http_requests_total (counter, labels: method, endpoint_slug, status_code), http_request_duration_seconds (histogram, labels: method, endpoint_slug, buckets=[0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5]), onboarding_attempts_total (counter, labels: env, role, outcome: success|token_invalid|pop_invalid|rate_limited), credential_issuance_total (counter, labels: credential_type, env, outcome), revocation_events_total (counter, labels: credential_type, env, severity), status_list_publish_duration_seconds (histogram, labels: env), chain_rpc_request_duration_seconds (histogram, labels: method, contract), chain_rpc_errors_total (counter, labels: method, error_type), chain_indexer_lag_blocks (gauge), audit_write_failure_total (counter), sentinel_status_gauge (gauge, labels: env, role, status: active|degraded|offline).

Step 6 - Metrics Exposition: Add starlette-prometheus or prometheus-client middleware. Expose GET /metrics (Prometheus scrape endpoint). No auth required on /metrics (network-level protection via Kubernetes NetworkPolicy). Disable access log for /metrics endpoint to avoid log noise.

Step 7 - GET /health/live: Returns { status: 'ok', service: 'discovery', version: app_version, uptime_seconds: int }. Always returns 200 if the process is running. Never performs I/O. Used as Kubernetes liveness probe.

Step 8 - GET /health/ready: Async checks: (1) DB: execute 'SELECT 1' with 2s timeout, (2) Redis: PING with 1s timeout, (3) Chain (optional): eth_blockNumber with 3s timeout — only included if chain_required=true. Response: { status: 'ready'|'not_ready', checks: { db: 'ok'|'error', redis: 'ok'|'error', chain: 'ok'|'error'|'skipped' } }. Returns 200 if status='ready', 503 if 'not_ready'. Used as Kubernetes readiness probe.

Step 9 - GET /health/detailed: Auth: operator. Deep health: includes pool stats (active_connections, idle_connections), Redis memory_used_mb, chain block number and lag, background task statuses (recent heartbeat_monitor run, status_list_publisher last run). Returns 200 with full diagnostic JSON.

Step 10 - Cardinality Discipline: endpoint_slug labels must use route template (e.g. 'POST /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/credentials') not the actual URL. DIDs and JTIs must never appear in metric labels. Audit: run prometheus-client-check or unit test that asserts no label contains a UUID or DID-like string.

files.services/discovery/src/discovery/telemet: OTel SDK init and manual span utilities

security_constraints: No secrets, tokens, DIDs, IPs, or user IDs in span attributes or metric labels
/health/ready must not expose internal error messages in response body — only 'ok'|'error' per check
/metrics endpoint must be restricted to internal network via Kubernetes NetworkPolicy — not internet-accessible
OTel exporter uses gRPC TLS when OTLP_INSECURE=false (default in prod)

acceptance_criteria: GET /health/ready returns 503 when DB is intentionally stopped (integration test)
After 100 POST /sentinels/onboard requests: http_requests_total counter shows count=100 for that endpoint slug
OTel trace appears in Jaeger/OTLP backend for credential issuance flow (manual span not just auto)
No metric label contains a string matching DID or UUID pattern (unit test)
